topic_title: Probabilistic systems — same input can give different outputs

citation_density.detail: All three resources are cited. Primary resource [1] appears in Sections 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 5.6, 5.7, 5.8, 6, 7, 8, and 10. Supporting resources [2] and [3] appear throughout Core Concepts, Real-World Patterns, and Best Practices. Citation density across Sections 5, 6, and 7 is well above the 60% threshold. No citation markers resolve to an undefined resource — all [1], [2], [3] map to entries in topic.resources.json.

soft_notes.detail: Appears once in an analogical sentence ('The "bag" in an AI language model holds a range of possible next words with associated likelihoods; the "card draw" is the sampling step that picks one of them'). Used as a named analogy target, not defined or taught. No internal mechanics explained. Technically not in concepts_introduced but treated as a bare name used to ground the sampling analogy rather than a forward-reference teaching event. Does not rise to a hard fail. A deferral pointer ('you will cover language models in a later topic') would remove the borderline status entirely.

sequence_drift.detail: All technical terms taught or defined in the corpus are present in this topic's concepts_introduced (probabilistic system, probability, uncertainty, randomness, sampling, output variation, temperature) or in prior topics' introduced vocabulary (computation, input/process/output, computable, computational thinking, defined steps, deterministic system, determinism, predictability, reproducibility, hidden inputs). The forward-reference violations from build cycle 1 (statistical model, machine learning classifiers, large language models, training data, random number generator defined as a component) are absent from the current corpus.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated 2,680 words is below the standard corpus minimum of 3,000 but above the hard-fail floor of 2,100 (3,000 × 0.70). Per SKILL.md, a hard fail requires word count more than 30% outside the range. At ~2,680 words the shortfall is approximately 11% below the floor — within tolerance. Not a blocking failure.
length_sanity.soft_finding_detail: Corpus at ~2,680 words is ~320 words short of the 3,000-word standard corpus minimum. Downstream reading artifact generation should compensate with richer examples or expanded walkthrough content to reach the 1,200–1,600-word reading target without padding the corpus artificially. Alternatively, the corpus builder may expand Section 5.5 or Section 5.8 with a second concrete example each.

topic_scope.detail: All substantive content stays within the headline 'Probabilistic systems — same input can give different outputs.' Temperature (Section 5.7) is correctly handled: it is in concepts_introduced, and the section opens with an explicit deferral for how language models work ('we will explore exactly how they work in later topics') before presenting temperature solely as a dial controlling output variation — fully in scope. The ML classifier content, LLM token-generation internals, statistical model definition, and training data / model bias content that appeared in build cycle 1 are absent. The 'AI language model' reference in Section 6 is a single analogical name used to anchor the sampling card-bag metaphor, not a lesson on LLMs. No scope_creep finding warranted.

beginner_readability.detail: All technical terms in this topic's concepts_introduced are defined at first use with a bold term followed by a plain-English meaning (probabilistic system §5.1, probability §5.2, uncertainty §5.2, randomness §5.3, sampling §5.4, output variation §5.8, temperature §5.7). No paragraph introduces three or more brand-new terms simultaneously — §5.2 introduces probability and uncertainty in separate paragraphs; §5.4 introduces sampling as the sole new term. No unexplained acronyms detected. Hedging phrases ('it is generally considered', 'many believe') not found; one passive 'is considered' in §7 Medical Diagnosis is within the tolerated limit. Sentence lengths are predominantly under 25 words. Concrete examples precede or accompany abstract definitions throughout (card-bag analogy, AI story generator, dice roll). Register is plain and learner-addressed ('you'). No distinct readability findings.

verdict: PASS. All six checks passed. The build-cycle-1 failures (sequence_drift on statistical model / ML classifiers / LLMs / training data / random number generator; scope_creep on §5.7 ML internals and §8 training-data best practice; beginner_readability escalated hard fail) are resolved in the current corpus — that content has been removed. One soft finding stands: estimated word count (~2,680) is below the 3,000-word standard corpus minimum but above the 30% hard-fail floor. No blocking revisions required. Optional: expand §5.5 or §5.8 by ~300 words with a second concrete example to reach the profile minimum and give the reading-builder richer source material. Soft note: adding a deferral pointer for 'AI language model' in §6 ('you will cover how language models work in a later topic') would cleanly resolve the borderline forward-reference status of that term.
